name: Centennial Bank
description_short: Centennial Bank is a family-owned FDIC-insured bank offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, and credit cards with high-yield rewards accounts.
description_long:
Centennial Bank has operated since 1916 as a family-owned community bank providing comprehensive financial services to personal and business customers. The bank recently absorbed The Bank of Jackson customers on March 23, expanding its customer base and service reach. Centennial Bank positions itself as an innovative regional bank focused on personalized, local service while maintaining modern digital banking capabilities.

The bank offers a full suite of personal banking products including multiple checking account tiers (Rewards Star, Star Checking, Gold Star Interest Checking, Rising Star Student, and 5 Star Protection Checking), savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, and IRAs. On the lending side, Centennial provides personal loans, auto loans, debt consolidation loans, home mortgages, and construction loans. Their business division includes business checking accounts, business savings, business loans, SBA loans, commercial real estate financing, and equipment financing. Additionally, Centennial offers credit cards for both personal and business use, featuring rewards programs including cash back options.

Centennial Bank distinguishes itself through high-yield rewards checking accounts (5.00% APY on balances up to $10,000) and a tiered rewards structure across multiple account types. The bank emphasizes accessibility with AudioEye-enabled website optimization and offers value-added services like Zelle transfers, mobile deposit, ATM fee refunds, and an Interactive Teller Machine (ITM) network. They've partnered with Travelers Insurance for auto and home insurance offerings and operate a pre-owned vehicle inventory for direct purchase by customers.

As a regional bank, Centennial Bank is FDIC-insured and regulated, providing safety guarantees on deposits. However, as a smaller regional institution, they may have more limited branch locations compared to national banks, and their service availability is likely concentrated in specific geographic areas. The website content does not provide specific information about fees, minimum balance requirements, or detailed APR ranges for loans, which are important considerations for prospective customers.
services: Personal checking accounts (Rewards Star, Star Checking, Gold Star Interest Checking, Rising Star Student, 5 Star Protection Checking)
Personal and business savings accounts, money market accounts, and CDs
Personal loans, auto loans, debt consolidation loans, and home mortgages
Business loans including SBA loans, equipment loans, agriculture loans, and commercial real estate loans
Personal and business credit cards with rewards programs
Mobile banking with mobile deposit and online bill pay
Zelle money transfers and mobile payments
Debit card services including CardSwap and Card Controls
Cash management and merchant card services for business customers
Auto and home insurance through Travelers partnership
Pre-owned vehicle sales directly to customers
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by U.S. government guarantee
High-yield checking account offering 5.00% APY on balances up to $10,000
Family-owned since 1916 with established community banking reputation
Comprehensive loan products including SBA loans, construction loans, and commercial real estate financing
Multiple rewards program tiers across checking, savings, and credit card products
Modern digital services including mobile banking with mobile deposit, Zelle transfers, and Interactive Teller Machines
Value-added partnerships including Travelers Insurance and pre-owned vehicle inventory
cons: As a regional bank, likely limited branch network compared to national banks
Website does not clearly display fee schedules or specific loan APR ranges
High-yield APY (5.00%) only applies to balances up to $10,000; amounts above earn 0.01% APY
Minimum balance requirements and qualification criteria for premium accounts not transparently listed on homepage
Recently absorbed The Bank of Jackson (March 23 transition), which may indicate ongoing systems integration and potential service disruptions
best_for: Local and regional small business owners seeking comprehensive commercial banking and SBA loan options
Consumers in Centennial Bank's service areas looking for high-yield checking accounts with rewards programs
Individuals and businesses seeking FDIC-insured banking with personalized community bank service
Customers transitioning from The Bank of Jackson who want to maintain local banking relationships
diagnosis: Centennial Bank is best for small business owners and regional customers seeking comprehensive commercial and personal banking with competitive rewards accounts and FDIC insurance. The main caveat is that as a regional bank, service availability is geographically limited, and the recent acquisition of The Bank of Jackson may affect service consistency during the integration period.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
